---
title: "Final_project_DATA2020"
author: "Zichen Zhao"
date: "2026-04-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
base_dir <- "/Users/akshaya/Desktop/data2020-spring2026/Paper_3__Movie_Bo__Office_Prediction_export/wikipredict_data_pack/sample_of_312"
movie_file <- file.path(base_dir, "sample_of_312")
predictor_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "wikipedia_predictors")

movies <- read.delim(movie_file, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

colnames(movies) <- c(
  "movie_id",
  "title",
  "wp_page_title",
  "revenue",
  "theaters",
  "release_date",
  "article_inception_movie_time"
)

movies$movie_id <- as.integer(movies$movie_id)
movies$revenue <- as.numeric(movies$revenue)
movies$theaters <- as.numeric(movies$theaters)
movies$release_date <- as.Date(movies$release_date)
movies$article_inception_movie_time <- as.numeric(movies$article_inception_movie_time)

head(movies)
dim(movies)
summary(movies$revenue)
summary(movies$theaters)
range(movies$release_date)
```
```{r}
# Check missing values in movie-level data
# Count missing values by column
movie_missing_count <- colSums(is.na(movies))

movie_missing_summary <- data.frame(
  variable = names(movie_missing_count),
  missing_count = as.integer(movie_missing_count),
  total_rows = nrow(movies),
  missing_percent = round(as.integer(movie_missing_count) / nrow(movies) * 100, 2)
)

movie_missing_summary

```
```{r}
check_predictor_missing <- function(movie_ids = movies$movie_id, pred_dir = predictor_dir) {
  out <- data.frame(
    movie_id = integer(),
    n_missing = integer(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (mid in movie_ids) {
    fpath <- file.path(pred_dir, as.character(mid))
    
    if (!file.exists(fpath)) next
    
    df <- read.delim(fpath, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    colnames(df) <- c("movie_time", "V", "U", "R", "E")
    
    miss_count <- sum(is.na(df))
    
    out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
      movie_id = mid,
      n_missing = miss_count,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  out
}

predictor_missing_df <- check_predictor_missing()
cat("Any predictor file with missing values?:", any(predictor_missing_df$n_missing > 0), "\n")
cat("Number of files with at least one missing value:",
    sum(predictor_missing_df$n_missing > 0), "\n")

head(predictor_missing_df[predictor_missing_df$n_missing > 0, ])
```
```{r}
# Check proportion of movies with no Wikipedia activity before t = -30
check_no_activity_before_t <- function(t = -30, pred_dir = predictor_dir) {
  
  result_list <- lapply(movies$movie_id, function(movie_id) {
    
    file_path <- file.path(pred_dir, as.character(movie_id))
    
    # Case 1: predictor file does not exist
    if (!file.exists(file_path)) {
      return(data.frame(
        movie_id = movie_id,
        has_file = FALSE,
        has_activity_before_t = FALSE,
        reason = "no predictor file"
      ))
    }
    
    df <- read.delim(
      file_path,
      sep = "\t",
      header = TRUE,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    colnames(df) <- c("movie_time", "V", "U", "R", "E")
    df$movie_time <- as.numeric(df$movie_time)
    
# Check whether this movie has any Wikipedia record before or at t
    has_activity <- any(!is.na(df$movie_time) & df$movie_time <= t)
    
    return(data.frame(
      movie_id = movie_id,
      has_file = TRUE,
      has_activity_before_t = has_activity,
      reason = ifelse(has_activity, "has activity before t", "no activity before t")
    ))
  })
  
  result_df <- do.call(rbind, result_list)
  
  return(result_df)
}


# Run check at t = -30
no_activity_check_t30 <- check_no_activity_before_t(t = -30)

# Summary table
summary_t30 <- data.frame(
  total_movies = nrow(no_activity_check_t30),
  no_activity_before_t = sum(no_activity_check_t30$has_activity_before_t == FALSE),
  has_activity_before_t = sum(no_activity_check_t30$has_activity_before_t == TRUE),
  no_activity_percent = round(mean(no_activity_check_t30$has_activity_before_t == FALSE) * 100, 2),
  has_activity_percent = round(mean(no_activity_check_t30$has_activity_before_t == TRUE) * 100, 2)
)

summary_t30


```

```{r}

#the predictor variable for specific time t=-30
load_predictors_for_movie <- function(movie_id, t = -30, pred_dir = predictor_dir) {
  file_path <- file.path(pred_dir, as.character(movie_id))
  
  df <- read.delim(file_path, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(df) <- c("movie_time", "V", "U", "R", "E")
  
  df$movie_time <- as.numeric(df$movie_time)
  df$V <- as.numeric(df$V)
  df$U <- as.numeric(df$U)
  df$R <- as.numeric(df$R)
  df$E <- as.numeric(df$E)
  
  df_sub <- df[df$movie_time <= t, ]
  
  if (nrow(df_sub) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      movie_id = movie_id,
      V = 0,
      U = 0,
      R = 0,
      E = 0
    ))
  }
  
  row <- df_sub[which.max(df_sub$movie_time), ]
  
  data.frame(
    movie_id = movie_id,
    V = row$V[1],
    U = row$U[1],
    R = row$R[1],
    E = row$E[1]
  )
}
#build dataset at t=-30
build_dataset_at_time_t <- function(t = -30) {
  predictor_list <- lapply(movies$movie_id, function(id) {
    load_predictors_for_movie(movie_id = id, t = t, pred_dir = predictor_dir)
  })
  
  predictor_df <- do.call(rbind, predictor_list)
  
  merged <- merge(movies, predictor_df, by = "movie_id", all.x = TRUE)
  
  merged <- merged[, c("movie_id", "title", "revenue", "theaters", "V", "U", "R", "E")]
  
  merged <- merged[complete.cases(merged[, c("revenue", "theaters")]), ]
  
  return(merged)
}
df_t <- build_dataset_at_time_t(t = -30)
head(df_t)
dim(df_t)
str(df_t)
```

```{r}
#Pearson correlation
compute_correlations <- function(df) {
  vars <- c("V", "U", "R", "E", "theaters")
  cors <- sapply(vars, function(x) cor(df[[x]], df$revenue))
  return(cors)
}
```

```{r}
#Figure 1
# D-G is accumulated to t=7
df_fig1 <- build_dataset_at_time_t(t = 7)


df_fig1 <- merge(
  df_fig1,
  movies[, c("movie_id", "article_inception_movie_time")],
  by = "movie_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)

log_revenue  <- log10(df_fig1$revenue[df_fig1$revenue > 0])
log_theaters <- log10(df_fig1$theaters[df_fig1$theaters > 0])
log_V        <- log10(df_fig1$V[df_fig1$V > 0])
log_U        <- log10(df_fig1$U[df_fig1$U > 0])
log_R        <- log10(df_fig1$R[df_fig1$R > 0])
log_E        <- log10(df_fig1$E[df_fig1$E > 0])
tc_values    <- df_fig1$article_inception_movie_time

par(mfrow = c(2, 4), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1), oma = c(0, 0, 2, 0))

# A
hist(tc_values,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "A",
     xlab = expression(t[c]),
     ylab = "Count of movies",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

# B
hist(log_revenue,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "B",
     xlab = expression(log[10](Y)),
     ylab = "",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

# C
hist(log_theaters,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "C",
     xlab = expression(log[10](T)),
     ylab = "",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

# D
hist(log_V,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "D",
     xlab = expression(log[10](V)),
     ylab = "Count of movies",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

# E
hist(log_U,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "E",
     xlab = expression(log[10](U)),
     ylab = "",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

# F
hist(log_R,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "F",
     xlab = expression(log[10](R)),
     ylab = "",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

# G
hist(log_E,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "G",
     xlab = expression(log[10](E)),
     ylab = "",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "gray40")

plot.new()

mtext("Figure 1(replicated)", outer = TRUE, cex = 1.2)



```



```{r}
#Pearson correlation over time
time_points <- seq(-400, 100, by = 1)

corr_tracking <- data.frame(
  t = integer(),
  V = numeric(),
  U = numeric(),
  R = numeric(),
  E = numeric(),
  T = numeric()
)

for (tt in time_points) {
  df_now <- build_dataset_at_time_t(t = tt)
  
  if (nrow(df_now) == 0) next
  
  cors <- compute_correlations(df_now)
  
  corr_tracking <- rbind(
    corr_tracking,
    data.frame(
      t = tt,
      V = cors["V"],
      U = cors["U"],
      R = cors["R"],
      E = cors["E"],
      T = cors["theaters"]
    )
  )
}
head(corr_tracking)

plot(
  corr_tracking$t, corr_tracking$V,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  xlab = "Time (day)",
  ylab = expression(r[j](t)),
  main = "Temporal evolution of Pearson correlation(replicated)"
)

lines(corr_tracking$t, corr_tracking$U, lwd = 2, col = 2)
lines(corr_tracking$t, corr_tracking$R, lwd = 2, col = 3)
lines(corr_tracking$t, corr_tracking$E, lwd = 2, col = 4)
lines(corr_tracking$t, corr_tracking$T, lwd = 2, col = "gray40", lty = 2)

legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c("V", "U", "R", "E", "T"),
  col = c(1, 2, 3, 4, "gray40"),
  lty = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 2),
  lwd = 2,
  bty = "n"
)

```

```{r}
# 10-fold CV R^2
cv_r2 <- function(df, feature_cols, k = 10, seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)
  n <- nrow(df)
  
  folds <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = n))
  r2_list <- c()
  
  formula_str <- paste("revenue ~", paste(feature_cols, collapse = " + "))
  model_formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_data <- df[folds != fold, ]
    test_data  <- df[folds == fold, ]
    
    model <- lm(model_formula, data = train_data)
    pred <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
    
    y_true <- test_data$revenue
    
    ss_res <- sum((y_true - pred)^2)
    ss_tot <- sum((y_true - mean(y_true))^2)
    r2 <- 1 - ss_res / ss_tot
    
    r2_list <- c(r2_list, r2)
  }
  
  return(list(
    mean_r2 = mean(r2_list),
    sd_r2 = sd(r2_list),
    all_r2 = r2_list
  ))
}
```

```{r}

# t = -30
t_point <- -30
df_t <- build_dataset_at_time_t(t = t_point)

head(df_t)
dim(df_t)

if (nrow(df_t) == 0) {
  stop("df_t is empty. Please check build_dataset_at_time_t() and file paths.")
}

cat(paste0("\nPearson correlations at t = ", t_point, ":\n"))
print(compute_correlations(df_t))

feature_sets <- list(
  c("theaters"),
  c("V"),
  c("V", "theaters"),
  c("U", "theaters"),
  c("V", "U", "R", "E"),
  c("V", "U", "R", "E", "theaters")
)

results <- data.frame(
  features = character(),
  mean_r2 = numeric(),
  sd_r2 = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (fs in feature_sets) {
  out <- cv_r2(df = df_t, feature_cols = fs, k = 10, seed = 42)
  
  results <- rbind(
    results,
    data.frame(
      features = paste(fs, collapse = ", "),
      mean_r2 = out$mean_r2,
      sd_r2 = out$sd_r2,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
}

results <- results[order(-results$mean_r2), ]

cat(paste0("\nR^2 results at t = ", t_point, ":\n"))
print(results)

```

```{r}
# Figure 3 R^2
time_points <- -30:10

tracking <- data.frame(
  t = integer(),
  model = character(),
  r2 = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

model_list <- list(
  "{T}" = c("theaters"),
  "{V}" = c("V"),
  "{V,T}" = c("V", "theaters"),
  "{U}" = c("U"),
  "{U,T}" = c("U", "theaters"),
  "{V,U,R,E}" = c("V", "U", "R", "E"),
  "{V,U,R,E,T}" = c("V", "U", "R", "E", "theaters")
)

for (tt in time_points) {
  df_now <- build_dataset_at_time_t(tt)
  
  for (model_name in names(model_list)) {
    fs <- model_list[[model_name]]
    out <- cv_r2(df_now, fs, k = 10, seed = 42)
    
    tracking <- rbind(tracking, data.frame(
      t = tt,
      model = model_name,
      r2 = out$mean_r2,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
}
model_colors <- c(
  "{T}" = "gray50",
  "{V}" = "blue",
  "{V,T}" = "blue",
  "{U}" = "pink",
  "{U,T}" = "red",
  "{V,U,R,E}" = "green3",
  "{V,U,R,E,T}" = "green3"
)

model_lty <- c(
  "{T}" = 2,          
  "{V}" = 3,           
  "{V,T}" = 1,      
  "{U}" = 3,
  "{U,T}" = 1,
  "{V,U,R,E}" = 3,
  "{V,U,R,E,T}" = 1
)

plot(
  NULL,
  xlim = range(tracking$t),
  ylim = c(0.35, 0.8),
  xlab = "Time (day)",
  ylab = expression(R^2(t)),
  main = " Figure 3: CV R^2 by Predictor Set(replicated) "
)

models <- names(model_list)

for (m in models) {
  sub <- tracking[tracking$model == m, ]
  lines(
    sub$t, sub$r2,
    type = "l",
    col = model_colors[m],
    lty = model_lty[m],
    lwd = 2
  )
}

legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = models,
  col = model_colors[models],
  lty = model_lty[models],
  lwd = 2,
  cex = 0.9,
  bty = "n"
)

```
 

```{r}

asur_dir <- "/Users/akshaya/Desktop/data2020-spring2026/Paper_3__Movie_Bo__Office_Prediction_export/wikipredict_data_pack/asur_huberman_sample_of_24"

# Check what is inside this folder
list.files(asur_dir)

# The actual data file is usually inside the folder with the same name
asur_file <- file.path(asur_dir, "asur_huberman_sample_of_24")

# Check path
asur_file
file.exists(asur_file)

# Read the 24-movie sample
asur24 <- read.delim(
  asur_file,
  sep = "\t",
  header = TRUE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

dim(asur24)
head(asur24)
colnames(asur24)



```







```{r}

base_dir_24 <- "/Users/akshaya/Desktop/data2020-spring2026/Paper_3__Movie_Bo__Office_Prediction_export/wikipredict_data_pack/asur_huberman_sample_of_24"
movie_file_24 <- file.path(base_dir_24, "asur_huberman_sample_of_24")
predictor_dir_24 <- file.path(base_dir_24, "wikipedia_predictors")

asur24 <- read.delim(
  movie_file_24,
  sep = "\t",
  header = TRUE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

colnames(asur24) <- c(
  "movie_id", "title", "wp_page_title",
  "revenue", "theaters", "release_date"
)

asur24$movie_id <- as.integer(asur24$movie_id)
asur24$revenue <- as.numeric(asur24$revenue)
asur24$theaters <- as.numeric(asur24$theaters)

load_predictors_24 <- function(mid, t, pred_dir = predictor_dir_24) {
  fpath <- file.path(pred_dir, as.character(mid))

  if (!file.exists(fpath)) {
    return(data.frame(movie_id = mid, V = NA, U = NA, R = NA, E = NA))
  }

  df <- read.delim(fpath, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(df) <- c("movie_time", "V", "U", "R", "E")
  df <- suppressWarnings(as.data.frame(lapply(df, as.numeric)))

  df_sub <- df[!is.na(df$movie_time) & df$movie_time <= t, ]

  if (nrow(df_sub) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(movie_id = mid, V = 0, U = 0, R = 0, E = 0))
  }

  row <- df_sub[which.max(df_sub$movie_time), ]

  data.frame(
    movie_id = mid,
    V = row$V,
    U = row$U,
    R = row$R,
    E = row$E
  )
}

build_dataset_24 <- function(t) {
  pred_list <- lapply(
    asur24$movie_id,
    function(mid) load_predictors_24(mid, t = t)
  )

  pred_df <- do.call(rbind, pred_list)

  merged <- merge(asur24, pred_df, by = "movie_id", all.x = TRUE)
  merged <- merged[, c("movie_id", "title", "revenue", "theaters", "V", "U", "R", "E")]
  merged <- merged[complete.cases(merged[, c("revenue", "theaters")]), ]

  merged
}


```

```{r}
time_points_fig4 <- -30:10

fig4_results <- data.frame(
  t = integer(),
  r2_wiki = numeric(),
  n = integer(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (tt in time_points_fig4) {
  df_24 <- build_dataset_24(t = tt)

  for (col in c("V", "U", "R", "E")) {
    df_24[[col]][is.na(df_24[[col]])] <- 0
  }

  n_obs <- nrow(df_24)

  if (n_obs < 5) {
    fig4_results <- rbind(
      fig4_results,
      data.frame(t = tt, r2_wiki = NA, n = n_obs)
    )
    next
  }

  model <- lm(revenue ~ V + U + R + E + theaters, data = df_24)
  pred <- predict(model, newdata = df_24)

  sse <- sum((df_24$revenue - pred)^2)
  sst <- sum((df_24$revenue - mean(df_24$revenue))^2)
  r2_val <- 1 - sse / sst

  fig4_results <- rbind(
    fig4_results,
    data.frame(t = tt, r2_wiki = r2_val, n = n_obs)
  )
}

print(fig4_results)



```
```{r}
#Figure 4

fig4_clean <- fig4_results[!is.na(fig4_results$r2_wiki), ]

plot(
  fig4_clean$t,
  fig4_clean$r2_wiki,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  col = "black",
  xlim = c(-30, 10),
  ylim = c(0.92, 1.00),
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = expression(R^2(t)),
  main = "Figure 4 (replicated)",
  las = 1
)

# Twitter benchmark line from t = 0 to t = 10
segments(
  x0 = 0,
  x1 = 10,
  y0 = 0.98,
  y1 = 0.98,
  col = "black",
  lty = 2,
  lwd = 2
)

# Vertical release-day line
abline(
  v = 0,
  col = "gray60",
  lty = 3,
  lwd = 1.5
)

legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = c("Wikipedia-based method", "Twitter-based method"),
  col = c("black", "black"),
  lty = c(1, 2),
  lwd = 2,
  bty = "n",
  cex = 0.9
)
```






```{r}
# full 312-movie sample at t = -30
df_full <- build_dataset_at_time_t(-30)

model_full <- lm(revenue ~ V + U + R + E + theaters, data = df_full)
df_full$predicted_revenue <- predict(model_full, newdata = df_full)

plot_full <- df_full[
  df_full$predicted_revenue > 0 &
  df_full$revenue > 0 &
  df_full$predicted_revenue >= 1e3 & df_full$predicted_revenue <= 1e9 &
  df_full$revenue >= 1e3 & df_full$revenue <= 1e9, ]

# 24-movie sample at t = -30
df_24 <- build_dataset_24(-30)

for (col in c("V", "U", "R", "E")) {
  df_24[[col]][is.na(df_24[[col]])] <- 0
}

model_24 <- lm(revenue ~ V + U + R + E + theaters, data = df_24)
df_24$predicted_revenue <- predict(model_24, newdata = df_24)

plot_24 <- df_24[
  df_24$predicted_revenue > 0 &
  df_24$revenue > 0 &
  df_24$predicted_revenue >= 1e3 & df_24$predicted_revenue <= 1e9 &
  df_24$revenue >= 1e3 & df_24$revenue <= 1e9, ]
```

```{r}

par(mar = c(5, 5, 2, 2), xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")

plot(
  plot_full$predicted_revenue,
  plot_full$revenue,
  log = "xy",
  type = "n",
  xlim = c(1e3, 1e9),
  ylim = c(1e3, 1e9),
  xlab = "Predicted revenue (USD)",
  ylab = "Actual revenue (USD)",
  asp = 1
)

# 312 movies
points(
  plot_full$predicted_revenue,
  plot_full$revenue,
  pch = 15,
  cex = 0.55,
  col = "gray30"
)

# 24 movies
points(
  plot_24$predicted_revenue,
  plot_24$revenue,
  pch = 15,
  cex = 0.55,
  col = "green"
)

# ideal prediction
abline(a = 0, b = 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

mtext("Wikipedia Prediction of Movie Box Office", side = 3, line = 0.6, adj = 1, cex = 0.8)

legend(
  "bottomright",
  inset = c(0.02, 0.02),
  legend = c(
    "Sample of 312 movies",
    "Sample of 24 movies (from Twitter-based study)",
    "Ideal prediction"
  ),
  pch = c(15, 15, NA),
  lty = c(NA, NA, 2),
  col = c("gray30", "green", "red"),
  pt.cex = c(0.65, 0.65, NA),
  cex = 0.82,
  bty = "n"
)


```

```{r extension}
# step1a.check the revenue distribution(highly skewed)
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(movies, aes(x = revenue)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Opening Weekend Revenue",
    x = "Opening weekend revenue",
    y = "Count"
  )
#log scale
ggplot(movies, aes(x = log(revenue))) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Log Opening Weekend Revenue",
    x = "Log opening weekend revenue",
    y = "Count"
  )

```


```{r}
#Linear regression assumption check
# Add log-transformed predictors
df_full$log_V <- log1p(df_full$V)
df_full$log_U <- log1p(df_full$U)
df_full$log_R <- log1p(df_full$R)
df_full$log_E <- log1p(df_full$E)
df_full$log_theaters <- log1p(df_full$theaters)
df_full$log_revenue <- log(df_full$revenue)

# Article-style raw OLS with log-transformed predictors
model_full_logX <- lm(
  revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
  data = df_full
)

# Extension: log-revenue OLS with log-transformed predictors
model_log_logX <- lm(
  log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
  data = df_full
)

summary(model_full_logX)
summary(model_log_logX)
```
```{r}
# Assumption plots for article-style raw OLS baseline
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_full)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
# Article-style raw OLS with log-transformed predictors
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_full_logX)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
# log-revenue OLS with log-transformed predictors
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model_log_logX)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

```

```{r}

# Independence assumption check based on the fitted log-log model
# Extract the exact rows used in model_log_logX
model_rows <- as.integer(rownames(model.frame(model_log_logX)))

# Build diagnostic dataframe directly from the fitted model
diag_ind <- data.frame(
  row_id = model_rows,
  fitted = fitted(model_log_logX),
  residual = resid(model_log_logX),
  standardized_residual = rstandard(model_log_logX)
)

# Add release date if it exists in df_full
if ("release_date" %in% names(df_full)) {
  diag_ind$release_date <- as.Date(df_full$release_date[model_rows])
}

# If release_date is not in df_full but exists in movies, use row order fallback
if (!"release_date" %in% names(diag_ind)) {
  if (exists("movies") && "release_date" %in% names(movies) && nrow(movies) >= max(model_rows)) {
    diag_ind$release_date <- as.Date(movies$release_date[model_rows])
  }
}

# Sort by release date if available
if ("release_date" %in% names(diag_ind) && sum(!is.na(diag_ind$release_date)) > 0) {
  diag_ind <- diag_ind[order(diag_ind$release_date), ]
}

# Create release order
diag_ind$release_order <- seq_len(nrow(diag_ind))

head(diag_ind)
dim(diag_ind)
```


```{r}
## Plot 1: Residuals vs Release Order
ggplot(diag_ind, aes(x = release_order, y = residual)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Independence Check: Residuals vs Release Order",
    x = "Release order",
    y = "Residuals from log-log model"
  )


```



```{r}
# Plot 2: Residuals vs Release Date
if ("release_date" %in% names(diag_ind) && sum(!is.na(diag_ind$release_date)) > 0) {
  
  ggplot(diag_ind, aes(x = release_date, y = residual)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = "Independence Check: Residuals vs Release Date",
      x = "Release date",
      y = "Residuals from log-log model"
    )
  
} else {
  print("release_date is not available, so this plot is skipped.")
}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Function to extract Cook's distance and related diagnostic values
get_cook_data <- function(model, data, model_name) {
  
  model_rows <- as.integer(rownames(model.frame(model)))
  
  out <- data.frame(
    row_id = model_rows,
    model = model_name,
    cooks_distance = cooks.distance(model),
    leverage = hatvalues(model),
    standardized_residual = rstandard(model)
  )
  
  # Add movie title if available
  if ("title" %in% names(data)) {
    out$title <- data$title[model_rows]
  } else if ("Title" %in% names(data)) {
    out$title <- data$Title[model_rows]
  } else {
    out$title <- paste("Movie", model_rows)
  }
  
  # Add revenue if available
  if ("revenue" %in% names(data)) {
    out$revenue <- data$revenue[model_rows]
  }
  
  return(out)
}

# Raw revenue OLS with log-transformed predictors
cook_raw <- get_cook_data(
  model = model_full_logX,
  data = df_full,
  model_name = "Raw revenue OLS"
)

# Log-revenue OLS with log-transformed predictors
cook_log <- get_cook_data(
  model = model_log_logX,
  data = df_full,
  model_name = "Log-log OLS"
)

cook_all <- rbind(cook_raw, cook_log)

head(cook_all)

```
```{r}
#cook's distance
get_cook_data <- function(model, data, model_name) {
  
  model_rows <- as.integer(rownames(model.frame(model)))
  
  out <- data.frame(
    row_id = model_rows,
    model = model_name,
    cooks_distance = cooks.distance(model),
    leverage = hatvalues(model),
    standardized_residual = rstandard(model)
  )
  
  # Add movie title
  if ("title" %in% names(data)) {
    out$title <- data$title[model_rows]
  } else if ("Title" %in% names(data)) {
    out$title <- data$Title[model_rows]
  } else {
    out$title <- paste("Movie", model_rows)
  }
  
  # Add revenue
  if ("revenue" %in% names(data)) {
    out$revenue <- data$revenue[model_rows]
  }
  
  return(out)
}

# Raw revenue OLS with log-transformed predictors
cook_raw <- get_cook_data(
  model = model_full_logX,
  data = df_full,
  model_name = "Raw revenue OLS"
)

# Log-revenue OLS with log-transformed predictors
cook_log <- get_cook_data(
  model = model_log_logX,
  data = df_full,
  model_name = "Log-log OLS"
)

cook_all <- rbind(cook_raw, cook_log)

head(cook_all)
#plot Cook's Distance
ggplot(cook_all, aes(x = row_id, y = cooks_distance)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(
    data = cook_all |>
      dplyr::group_by(model) |>
      dplyr::summarise(threshold = 4 / dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop"),
    aes(yintercept = threshold),
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ model, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Cook's Distance for Raw OLS and Log-log OLS",
    x = "Movie index",
    y = "Cook's distance"
  )

```
```{r}
#find that influence observation
library(knitr)

# Extract Cook's distance for log-log model
model_rows <- as.integer(rownames(model.frame(model_log_logX)))

cook_log_detail <- data.frame(
  row_id = model_rows,
  cooks_distance = cooks.distance(model_log_logX),
  leverage = hatvalues(model_log_logX),
  standardized_residual = rstandard(model_log_logX)
)

# Add movie information
if ("title" %in% names(df_full)) {
  cook_log_detail$title <- df_full$title[model_rows]
} else if ("Title" %in% names(df_full)) {
  cook_log_detail$title <- df_full$Title[model_rows]
}

cook_log_detail$revenue <- df_full$revenue[model_rows]
cook_log_detail$V <- df_full$V[model_rows]
cook_log_detail$U <- df_full$U[model_rows]
cook_log_detail$R <- df_full$R[model_rows]
cook_log_detail$E <- df_full$E[model_rows]
cook_log_detail$theaters <- df_full$theaters[model_rows]

# Common Cook's distance threshold
cook_threshold <- 4 / nobs(model_log_logX)

top_cook_log <- cook_log_detail %>%
  arrange(desc(cooks_distance)) %>%
  mutate(
    above_threshold = cooks_distance > cook_threshold
  ) %>%
  slice(1:10)

kable(top_cook_log, digits = 4)
cook_threshold


```

```{r}
# Identify the most influential observation in log-log model
top_row <- top_cook_log$row_id[1]

top_row
df_full[top_row, ]

# Full log-log model
model_log_logX_full <- lm(
  log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
  data = df_full
)

# Remove the most influential observation
df_without_top <- df_full[-top_row, ]

model_log_logX_without_top <- lm(
  log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
  data = df_without_top
)

summary(model_log_logX_full)
summary(model_log_logX_without_top)
#compare any big changese happened in coefficients
coef_compare <- data.frame(
  term = names(coef(model_log_logX_full)),
  full_model = coef(model_log_logX_full),
  without_top = coef(model_log_logX_without_top)
)

coef_compare$absolute_change <- coef_compare$without_top - coef_compare$full_model
coef_compare$percent_change <- 100 * coef_compare$absolute_change / abs(coef_compare$full_model)

knitr::kable(coef_compare, digits = 4)
```



```{r}
library(dplyr)
# Prepare log-model dataset
df_ms <- build_dataset_at_time_t(-30)


# Log-transform outcome and predictors
df_ms$log_revenue <- log(df_ms$revenue)

df_ms$log_V <- log1p(df_ms$V)
df_ms$log_U <- log1p(df_ms$U)
df_ms$log_R <- log1p(df_ms$R)
df_ms$log_E <- log1p(df_ms$E)
df_ms$log_theaters <- log1p(df_ms$theaters)

summary(df_ms$log_revenue)
dim(df_ms)
# 4 log model 
candidate_formulas <- list(
  
  "Log Model A: Wikipedia only" =
    log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E,
  
  "Log Model B: Wikipedia + theaters" =
    log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
  
  "Log Model C: Reduced no rigor" =
    log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_E + log_theaters,
 #V has high relative with the revenue before movies release 
   "Log Model D: Simple benchmark"=
     log_revenue ~ log_V + log_theaters
)


# Create cross-validation folds
set.seed(529)

make_folds <- function(data, k = 5) {
  
  n <- nrow(data)
  
  if ("release_date" %in% names(data)) {
    
    data$release_date <- as.Date(data$release_date)
    data <- data[order(data$release_date), ]
    
    folds <- list(
      fold1 = list(
        train = 1:floor(0.50 * n),
        test = (floor(0.50 * n) + 1):floor(0.65 * n)
      ),
      fold2 = list(
        train = 1:floor(0.65 * n),
        test = (floor(0.65 * n) + 1):floor(0.80 * n)
      ),
      fold3 = list(
        train = 1:floor(0.80 * n),
        test = (floor(0.80 * n) + 1):n
      )
    )
    
  } else {
    
    fold_id <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = n))
    
    folds <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
      list(
        train = which(fold_id != i),
        test = which(fold_id == i)
      )
    })
    
    names(folds) <- paste0("fold", 1:k)
  }
  
  return(list(data = data, folds = folds))
}

fold_object <- make_folds(df_ms, k = 10)

df_ms_cv <- fold_object$data
folds <- fold_object$folds

names(folds)

# Cross-validation function
run_cv_for_model <- function(data, folds, formula_obj, model_name) {
  
  metrics_all <- data.frame()
  predictions_all <- data.frame()
  
  for (fold_name in names(folds)) {
    
    train_idx <- folds[[fold_name]]$train
    test_idx <- folds[[fold_name]]$test
    
    train_data <- data[train_idx, ]
    test_data <- data[test_idx, ]
    
    vars_needed <- unique(c(all.vars(formula_obj), "revenue"))
    
    train_data <- train_data[complete.cases(train_data[, vars_needed]), ]
    test_data <- test_data[complete.cases(test_data[, vars_needed]), ]
    
    fit <- lm(formula_obj, data = train_data)
    
    pred_log <- predict(fit, newdata = test_data)
    pred_revenue <- exp(pred_log)
    
    actual_log <- test_data$log_revenue
    actual_revenue <- test_data$revenue
    
    Log_RMSE <- sqrt(mean((actual_log - pred_log)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
    Log_MAE <- mean(abs(actual_log - pred_log), na.rm = TRUE)
    
    MAE <- mean(abs(actual_revenue - pred_revenue), na.rm = TRUE)
    RMSE <- sqrt(mean((actual_revenue - pred_revenue)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
    RMSLE <- sqrt(mean((log1p(actual_revenue) - log1p(pred_revenue))^2, na.rm = TRUE))
    
    metrics_fold <- data.frame(
      model = model_name,
      fold = fold_name,
      n_test = nrow(test_data),
      Log_RMSE = Log_RMSE,
      Log_MAE = Log_MAE,
      MAE = MAE,
      RMSE = RMSE,
      RMSLE = RMSLE
    )
    
    predictions_fold <- data.frame(
      model = model_name,
      fold = fold_name,
      actual_revenue = actual_revenue,
      predicted_revenue = pred_revenue,
      actual_log_revenue = actual_log,
      predicted_log_revenue = pred_log
    )
    
    metrics_all <- rbind(metrics_all, metrics_fold)
    predictions_all <- rbind(predictions_all, predictions_fold)
  }
  
  return(list(
    metrics = metrics_all,
    predictions = predictions_all
  ))
}
```
```{r}
#log model selection continuing...

cv_metrics_all <- data.frame()
cv_predictions_all <- data.frame()

for (model_name in names(candidate_formulas)) {
  
  result <- run_cv_for_model(
    data = df_ms_cv,
    folds = folds,
    formula_obj = candidate_formulas[[model_name]],
    model_name = model_name
  )
  
  cv_metrics_all <- rbind(cv_metrics_all, result$metrics)
  cv_predictions_all <- rbind(cv_predictions_all, result$predictions)
}

head(cv_metrics_all)
names(cv_metrics_all)
#summarize CV results
cv_summary <- cv_metrics_all %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    n_folds = n(),
    mean_Log_RMSE = mean(Log_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_Log_RMSE = sd(Log_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    mean_Log_MAE = mean(Log_MAE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RMSLE = mean(RMSLE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_MAE = mean(MAE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RMSE = mean(RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_Log_RMSE)

cv_summary

```
```{r}
#BIC for log limited model selection
get_model_info <- function(model_name, formula_obj, data) {
  
  fit <- lm(formula_obj, data = data)
  fit_summary <- summary(fit)
  
  data.frame(
    model = model_name,
    n = nobs(fit),
    num_predictors = length(attr(terms(formula_obj), "term.labels")),
    AIC = AIC(fit),
    BIC = BIC(fit),
    adj_R2 = fit_summary$adj.r.squared
  )
}

bic_results <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(names(candidate_formulas), function(model_name) {
    get_model_info(
      model_name = model_name,
      formula_obj = candidate_formulas[[model_name]],
      data = df_ms    # ← this is what you actually built
    )
  })
)

bic_results <- bic_results %>%
  arrange(BIC) %>%
  mutate(delta_BIC = BIC - min(BIC))

print(bic_results)

```
```{r}
model_selection_table <- cv_summary %>%
  left_join(
    bic_results %>%
      select(model, num_predictors, AIC, BIC, delta_BIC, adj_R2),
    by = "model"
  ) %>%
  arrange(BIC)

knitr::kable(model_selection_table, digits = 3)

```

```{r}
# Gamma GLM Model B and Model C
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

# Model B: full Wikipedia + theaters model
gamma_formula_b <- revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters

# Model C: reduced no-rigor model
gamma_formula_c <- revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_E + log_theaters

gamma_formulas <- list(
  "Gamma Model B: Full Wikipedia + theaters" = gamma_formula_b,
  "Gamma Model C: Reduced no rigor" = gamma_formula_c
)

gamma_formulas
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# Prepare common dataset for Gamma BIC comparison
# ============================================================

vars_gamma_all <- unique(unlist(lapply(gamma_formulas, all.vars)))

missing_vars <- setdiff(vars_gamma_all, names(df_ms_cv))
missing_vars

df_gamma_common <- df_ms_cv %>%
  filter(
    complete.cases(across(all_of(vars_gamma_all))),
    revenue > 0
  )

dim(df_gamma_common)
summary(df_gamma_common$revenue)
```

```{r}
#Fit Gamma GLM B and C with log link

gamma_model_b <- glm(
  gamma_formula_b,
  data = df_gamma_common,
  family = Gamma(link = "log"),
  control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
)

gamma_model_c <- glm(
  gamma_formula_c,
  data = df_gamma_common,
  family = Gamma(link = "log"),
  control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
)

summary(gamma_model_b)
summary(gamma_model_c)

```
```{r}
#Fit Gamma GLM B and C with log link

gamma_model_b <- glm(
  gamma_formula_b,
  data = df_gamma_common,
  family = Gamma(link = "log"),
  control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
)

gamma_model_c <- glm(
  gamma_formula_c,
  data = df_gamma_common,
  family = Gamma(link = "log"),
  control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
)

summary(gamma_model_b)
summary(gamma_model_c)

```
```{r}
#BIC / AIC comparison for Gamma B and C

gamma_bic_bc <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(names(gamma_formulas), function(model_name) {
    
    fit <- glm(
      gamma_formulas[[model_name]],
      data = df_gamma_common,
      family = Gamma(link = "log"),
      control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
    )
    
    data.frame(
      model = model_name,
      n = nobs(fit),
      num_predictors = length(attr(terms(gamma_formulas[[model_name]]), "term.labels")),
      converged = fit$converged,
      AIC = AIC(fit),
      BIC = BIC(fit)
    )
  })
)

gamma_bic_bc <- gamma_bic_bc %>%
  arrange(BIC) %>%
  mutate(delta_BIC = BIC - min(BIC, na.rm = TRUE))

knitr::kable(gamma_bic_bc, digits = 3)


```
```{r}
#Cross-validation function for Gamma GLM
run_gamma_model_cv <- function(data, folds, formula_obj, model_name) {
  
  metrics_all <- data.frame()
  predictions_all <- data.frame()
  
  for (fold_name in names(folds)) {
    
    train_idx <- folds[[fold_name]]$train
    test_idx <- folds[[fold_name]]$test
    
    train_data <- data[train_idx, ]
    test_data <- data[test_idx, ]
    
    vars_needed <- all.vars(formula_obj)
    
    train_data <- train_data %>%
      filter(
        complete.cases(across(all_of(vars_needed))),
        revenue > 0
      )
    
    test_data <- test_data %>%
      filter(
        complete.cases(across(all_of(vars_needed))),
        revenue > 0
      )
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      glm(
        formula_obj,
        data = train_data,
        family = Gamma(link = "log"),
        control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
      ),
      error = function(e) {
        return(NULL)
      }
    )
    
    if (is.null(fit)) {
      next
    }
    
    pred_revenue <- predict(
      fit,
      newdata = test_data,
      type = "response"
    )
    
    pred_safe <- pmax(pred_revenue, 1e-6)
    
    metrics_fold <- data.frame(
      model = model_name,
      fold = fold_name,
      n_test = nrow(test_data),
      converged = fit$converged,
      bad_predictions = sum(pred_revenue <= 0, na.rm = TRUE),
      
      Log_RMSE = sqrt(mean(
        (log(test_data$revenue) - log(pred_safe))^2,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )),
      
      Log_MAE = mean(
        abs(log(test_data$revenue) - log(pred_safe)),
        na.rm = TRUE
      ),
      
      RMSLE = sqrt(mean(
        (log1p(test_data$revenue) - log1p(pred_safe))^2,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )),
      
      MAE = mean(
        abs(test_data$revenue - pred_revenue),
        na.rm = TRUE
      ),
      
      RMSE = sqrt(mean(
        (test_data$revenue - pred_revenue)^2,
        na.rm = TRUE
      ))
    )
    
    predictions_fold <- data.frame(
      model = model_name,
      fold = fold_name,
      actual_revenue = test_data$revenue,
      predicted_revenue = pred_revenue,
      predicted_revenue_safe = pred_safe
    )
    
    metrics_all <- rbind(metrics_all, metrics_fold)
    predictions_all <- rbind(predictions_all, predictions_fold)
  }
  
  return(list(
    metrics = metrics_all,
    predictions = predictions_all
  ))
}
```

```{r}
# Run CV for Gamma Model B and C

gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all <- data.frame()
gamma_bc_cv_predictions_all <- data.frame()

for (model_name in names(gamma_formulas)) {
  
  result <- run_gamma_model_cv(
    data = df_ms_cv,
    folds = folds,
    formula_obj = gamma_formulas[[model_name]],
    model_name = model_name
  )
  
  gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all <- rbind(
    gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all,
    result$metrics
  )
  
  gamma_bc_cv_predictions_all <- rbind(
    gamma_bc_cv_predictions_all,
    result$predictions
  )
}

head(gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all)
names(gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all)

```
```{r}
#Summarize Gamma B and C CV results

gamma_bc_cv_summary <- gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    n_folds = n(),
    mean_Log_RMSE = mean(Log_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_Log_RMSE = sd(Log_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    mean_Log_MAE = mean(Log_MAE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RMSLE = mean(RMSLE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_MAE = mean(MAE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RMSE = mean(RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_bad_predictions = sum(bad_predictions, na.rm = TRUE),
    all_converged = all(converged),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_Log_RMSE)

knitr::kable(gamma_bc_cv_summary, digits = 3)

```
```{r}
#Summarize Gamma B and C CV results

gamma_bc_cv_summary <- gamma_bc_cv_metrics_all %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    n_folds = n(),
    mean_Log_RMSE = mean(Log_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_Log_RMSE = sd(Log_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    mean_Log_MAE = mean(Log_MAE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RMSLE = mean(RMSLE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_MAE = mean(MAE, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RMSE = mean(RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_bad_predictions = sum(bad_predictions, na.rm = TRUE),
    all_converged = all(converged),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_Log_RMSE)

knitr::kable(gamma_bc_cv_summary, digits = 3)

```
```{r}
#Combine Gamma BIC and CV results

gamma_model_selection_table <- gamma_bc_cv_summary %>%
  left_join(
    gamma_bic_bc %>%
      select(model, num_predictors, AIC, BIC, delta_BIC),
    by = "model"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_Log_RMSE)

knitr::kable(gamma_model_selection_table, digits = 3)

```
```{r}
# Final Gamma GLM model: Model B
final_gamma_model_b <- glm(
  revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
  data = df_gamma_common,
  family = Gamma(link = "log"),
  control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
)

summary(final_gamma_model_b)

df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b <- predict(
  final_gamma_model_b,
  newdata = df_gamma_common,
  type = "response"
)

df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b_safe <- pmax(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b, 1e-6)

summary(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b)
```

```{r}
df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b <- predict(
  final_gamma_model_b,
  newdata = df_gamma_common,
  type = "response"
)

df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b_safe <- pmax(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b, 1e-6)

summary(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b)
#Final Gamma Model B metrics
gamma_b_final_metrics <- data.frame(
  model = "Final Gamma Model B: V + U + R+ E + theaters",
  
  Log_RMSE = sqrt(mean(
    (log(df_gamma_common$revenue) - log(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b_safe))^2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )),
  
  Log_MAE = mean(
    abs(log(df_gamma_common$revenue) - log(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b_safe)),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),
  
  RMSLE = sqrt(mean(
    (log1p(df_gamma_common$revenue) - log1p(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b_safe))^2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )),
  
  MAE = mean(
    abs(df_gamma_common$revenue - df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),
  
  RMSE = sqrt(mean(
    (df_gamma_common$revenue - df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b)^2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )),
  
  bad_predictions = sum(df_gamma_common$pred_gamma_b <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)
)

gamma_b_final_metrics
```
```{r}
ggplot(
  df_gamma_common,
  aes(x = revenue, y = pred_gamma_b_safe)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_abline(
    intercept = 0,
    slope = 1,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Final Gamma Model B: Actual vs Predicted Revenue",
    x = "Actual revenue, log scale",
    y = "Predicted revenue, log scale"
  )

```
```{r}
df_gamma_common$gamma_b_fitted <- fitted(final_gamma_model_b)

df_gamma_common$gamma_b_deviance_resid <- residuals(
  final_gamma_model_b,
  type = "deviance"
)

ggplot(
  df_gamma_common,
  aes(x = gamma_b_fitted, y = gamma_b_deviance_resid)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Final Gamma Model B: Deviance Residuals vs Fitted Values",
    x = "Fitted revenue, log scale",
    y = "Deviance residuals"
  )

```
```{r}
#Post-prediction revenue quartile analysis
# Final Gamma Model B

df_gamma_common$revenue_quartile <- cut(
  df_gamma_common$revenue,
  breaks = quantile(
    df_gamma_common$revenue,
    probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = c(
    "Q1 lowest revenue",
    "Q2",
    "Q3",
    "Q4 highest revenue"
  )
)

gamma_b_quartile_metrics <- df_gamma_common %>%
  group_by(revenue_quartile) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    
    mean_actual = mean(revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_predicted = mean(pred_gamma_b, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    bias = mean(pred_gamma_b - revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    Log_RMSE = sqrt(mean(
      (log(revenue) - log(pred_gamma_b_safe))^2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    
    RMSLE = sqrt(mean(
      (log1p(revenue) - log1p(pred_gamma_b_safe))^2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    
    MAE = mean(
      abs(revenue - pred_gamma_b),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    RMSE = sqrt(mean(
      (revenue - pred_gamma_b)^2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(gamma_b_quartile_metrics, digits = 3)

```

```{r}

#Plot Gamma Model B error by revenue quartile

ggplot(
  gamma_b_quartile_metrics,
  aes(x = revenue_quartile, y = RMSLE)
) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Final Gamma Model B: Prediction Error by Revenue Quartile",
    x = "Revenue quartile, used only after prediction",
    y = "RMSLE"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

```
```{r}
# Quartile comparison across Raw OLS, Log-log OLS B/C, and Gamma B
# Prepare common dataset

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

# Variables needed for all models
compare_vars <- c(
  "revenue",
  "V", "U", "R", "E", "theaters",
  "log_revenue",
  "log_V", "log_U", "log_R", "log_E", "log_theaters"
)

# Check missing variables
setdiff(compare_vars, names(df_ms_cv))

# Use the same complete-case dataset for all models
df_compare <- df_ms_cv %>%
  filter(
    complete.cases(across(all_of(compare_vars))),
    revenue > 0
  )

dim(df_compare)
summary(df_compare$revenue)
```
```{r}
#Create folds on the common comparison dataset

set.seed(529)

if (!exists("make_folds")) {
  
  make_folds <- function(data, k = 10) {
    
    n <- nrow(data)
    fold_id <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = n))
    
    folds <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
      list(
        train = which(fold_id != i),
        test = which(fold_id == i)
      )
    })
    
    names(folds) <- paste0("fold", 1:k)
    
    return(list(data = data, folds = folds))
  }
}

fold_object_compare <- make_folds(df_compare, k = 10)

df_compare_cv <- fold_object_compare$data
folds_compare <- fold_object_compare$folds

names(folds_compare)
dim(df_compare_cv)
# Function to generate CV predictions for each model
predict_one_model_cv <- function(data, folds, model_name) {
  
  predictions_all <- data.frame()
  
  for (fold_name in names(folds)) {
    
    train_idx <- folds[[fold_name]]$train
    test_idx <- folds[[fold_name]]$test
    
    train_data <- data[train_idx, ]
    test_data <- data[test_idx, ]
    
    if (model_name == "Raw OLS baseline") {
      
      fit <- lm(
        revenue ~ V + U + R + E + theaters,
        data = train_data
      )
      
      pred_revenue <- predict(fit, newdata = test_data)
    }
    
    if (model_name == "Log-log OLS B: Full model") {
      
      fit <- lm(
        log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
        data = train_data
      )
      
      pred_log <- predict(fit, newdata = test_data)
      pred_revenue <- exp(pred_log)
    }
    
    if (model_name == "Log-log OLS C: Reduced no rigor") {
      
      fit <- lm(
        log_revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_E + log_theaters,
        data = train_data
      )
      
      pred_log <- predict(fit, newdata = test_data)
      pred_revenue <- exp(pred_log)
    }
    
    if (model_name == "Gamma GLM B: Full model") {
      
      fit <- glm(
        revenue ~ log_V + log_U + log_R + log_E + log_theaters,
        data = train_data,
        family = Gamma(link = "log"),
        control = glm.control(maxit = 100)
      )
      
      pred_revenue <- predict(
        fit,
        newdata = test_data,
        type = "response"
      )
    }
    
    # Revenue cannot be negative.
    # This is especially relevant for Raw OLS.
    bad_predictions <- sum(pred_revenue <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    pred_revenue_safe <- pmax(pred_revenue, 1e-6)
    
    predictions_fold <- data.frame(
      row_id = test_idx,
      fold = fold_name,
      model = model_name,
      actual_revenue = test_data$revenue,
      predicted_revenue = pred_revenue,
      predicted_revenue_safe = pred_revenue_safe,
      bad_predictions = bad_predictions
    )
    
    predictions_all <- rbind(predictions_all, predictions_fold)
  }
  
  return(predictions_all)
}
```
```{r}
# Generate CV predictions for all models
models_to_compare <- c(
  "Raw OLS baseline",
  "Log-log OLS B: Full model",
  "Log-log OLS C: Reduced no rigor",
  "Gamma GLM B: Full model"
)

all_cv_predictions <- data.frame()

for (m in models_to_compare) {
  
  temp_pred <- predict_one_model_cv(
    data = df_compare_cv,
    folds = folds_compare,
    model_name = m
  )
  
  all_cv_predictions <- rbind(all_cv_predictions, temp_pred)
}

head(all_cv_predictions)
dim(all_cv_predictions)

#Create post-prediction revenue quartiles
quartile_breaks <- quantile(
  df_compare_cv$revenue,
  probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

all_cv_predictions$revenue_quartile <- cut(
  all_cv_predictions$actual_revenue,
  breaks = quartile_breaks,
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = c(
    "Q1 lowest revenue",
    "Q2",
    "Q3",
    "Q4 highest revenue"
  )
)

table(all_cv_predictions$model, all_cv_predictions$revenue_quartile)
```

```{r}
# Quartile-level prediction metrics
quartile_metrics_all <- all_cv_predictions %>%
  group_by(model, revenue_quartile) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    
    mean_actual = mean(actual_revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_predicted = mean(predicted_revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    bias = mean(predicted_revenue - actual_revenue, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    Log_RMSE = sqrt(mean(
      (log(actual_revenue) - log(predicted_revenue_safe))^2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    
    RMSLE = sqrt(mean(
      (log1p(actual_revenue) - log1p(predicted_revenue_safe))^2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    
    MAE = mean(
      abs(actual_revenue - predicted_revenue),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    
    RMSE = sqrt(mean(
      (actual_revenue - predicted_revenue)^2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )),
    
    bad_predictions = sum(predicted_revenue <= 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(revenue_quartile, model)

knitr::kable(quartile_metrics_all, digits = 3)
```
```{r}
# Improvement relative to Raw OLS baseline

raw_reference <- quartile_metrics_all %>%
  filter(model == "Raw OLS baseline") %>%
  select(
    revenue_quartile,
    raw_RMSLE = RMSLE,
    raw_MAE = MAE,
    raw_RMSE = RMSE
  )

quartile_improvement <- quartile_metrics_all %>%
  left_join(raw_reference, by = "revenue_quartile") %>%
  mutate(
    RMSLE_reduction = raw_RMSLE - RMSLE,
    RMSLE_percent_reduction = 100 * (raw_RMSLE - RMSLE) / raw_RMSLE,
    
    MAE_reduction = raw_MAE - MAE,
    MAE_percent_reduction = 100 * (raw_MAE - MAE) / raw_MAE,
    
    RMSE_reduction = raw_RMSE - RMSE,
    RMSE_percent_reduction = 100 * (raw_RMSE - RMSE) / raw_RMSE
  ) %>%
  arrange(revenue_quartile, desc(RMSLE_percent_reduction))

knitr::kable(quartile_improvement, digits = 3)

```
```{r}
#Plot RMSLE by revenue quartile
ggplot(
  quartile_metrics_all,
  aes(x = revenue_quartile, y = RMSLE, fill = model)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "CV RMSLE by Revenue Quartile",
    x = "Revenue quartile, used only after prediction",
    y = "RMSLE",
    fill = "Model"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
# Plot prediction bias by revenue quartile
ggplot(
  quartile_metrics_all,
  aes(x = revenue_quartile, y = bias, fill = model)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-validated Prediction Bias by Revenue Quartile",
    x = "Revenue quartile, used only after prediction",
    y = "Mean bias: predicted - actual",
    fill = "Model"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))


```

```{r}
# Q1 and Q2 only table
q1q2_metrics <- quartile_metrics_all %>%
  filter(revenue_quartile %in% c("Q1 lowest revenue", "Q2")) %>%
  arrange(revenue_quartile, model)

knitr::kable(q1q2_metrics, digits = 3)
ggplot(
  q1q2_metrics,
  aes(x = revenue_quartile, y = RMSLE, fill = model)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(RMSLE, 2)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 3
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "CV RMSLE for Q1 and Q2 Revenue Movies",
    x = "Revenue quartile",
    y = "RMSLE",
    fill = "Model"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

q1q2_bias <- quartile_metrics_all %>%
  filter(revenue_quartile %in% c("Q1 lowest revenue", "Q2")) %>%
  arrange(revenue_quartile, model)

q1q2_bias
ggplot(
  q1q2_bias,
  aes(x = revenue_quartile, y = bias, fill = model)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = comma(round(bias, 0)),
      vjust = ifelse(bias >= 0, -0.3, 1.2)
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Zoomed-in CV Prediction Bias for Q1 and Q2",
    x = "Revenue quartile",
    y = "Mean bias: predicted - actual",
    fill = "Model"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

```
